question: Where does the assistant call a language model backend to answer a user question?
ground_truth_note: crates/ai/ is the AI library (LLMId, api_keys, agent module). app/src/ai/llms.rs has the HTTP API calls. app/src/ai_assistant/requests.rs is the (deprecated) caller. app/src/ai/agent_conversations_model.rs is the modern conversation runner. All four are valid answers depending on interpretation of "call a language model backend".

question: Where is the screenshot-and-click tool that lets the model interact with the desktop?
How does the in-line text editor handle cursor movement and keystroke input?
Where are language server protocol messages serialized and dispatched to and from a child process?
How are vim normal-mode commands like h, j, k, l interpreted into editor actions?
Where is microphone audio captured and sent for speech-to-text transcription?
How does shell command autocompletion generate suggestions while the user is typing?
Where is the ranking algorithm that scores fuzzy matches between a query and candidate entries?
How are user preferences persisted between sessions and reloaded at startup?
Where does the client establish a websocket connection to the server and reconnect after a drop?
How are sensitive credentials encrypted before they are written to disk?
Where is markdown rendered into formatted terminal output blocks?
Where is the keyboard-shortcut driven command palette opened and ranked?
How does the application sign a user in and store their session token?
Where is the user's subscription tier checked before unlocking paid features?
ground_truth_note: app/src/billing/ contains only denial modal UI (shared_objects_creation_denied_*). Real subscription-tier-check logic is in app/src/settings_view/billing_and_usage_page.rs (top hit), app/src/auth/auth_state.rs, app/src/settings_view/features_page.rs, app/src/settings_view/main_page.rs.
